2603.17123.title: Security Assessment and Mitigation Strategies for Large Language Models: A Comprehensive Defensive Framework
2603.17123.paper_summary: Large Language Models increasingly power critical infrastructure from healthcare to finance, yet their vulnerability to adversarial manipulation threatens system integrity and user safety. Despite growing deployment, no comprehensive comparative security assessment exists across major LLM architectures, leaving organizations unable to quantify risk or select appropriately secure LLMs for sensitive applications. This research addresses this gap by establishing a standardized vulnerability assessment framework and developing a multi-layered defensive system to protect against identified threats. We systematically evaluate five widely-deployed LLM families GPT-4, GPT-3.5 Turbo, Claude-3 Haiku, LLaMA-2-70B, and Gemini-2.5-pro against 10,000 adversarial prompts spanning six attack categories. Our assessment reveals critical security disparities, with vulnerability rates ranging from 11.9\% to 29.8\%, demonstrating that LLM capability does not correlate with security robustness. To mitigate these risks, we develop a production-ready defensive framework achieving 83\% average detection accuracy with only 5\% false positives. These results demonstrate that systematic security assessment combined with external defensive measures provides a viable path toward safer LLM deployment in production environments.
2603.17123.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地驅動著從醫療到金融的關鍵基礎設施，但它們對對抗性操控的脆弱性威脅著系統的完整性和用戶的安全。儘管部署不斷增加，但目前尚無針對主要LLM架構的全面比較安全評估，這使得組織無法量化風險或為敏感應用選擇適當安全的LLM。本研究通過建立標準化的脆弱性評估框架並開發多層防禦系統來保護免受已識別威脅的影響，來填補這一空白。我們系統地評估了五個廣泛部署的LLM家族：GPT-4、GPT-3.5 Turbo、Claude-3 Haiku、LLaMA-2-70B和Gemini-2.5-pro，針對10,000個涵蓋六種攻擊類別的對抗性提示。我們的評估揭示了關鍵的安全差異，脆弱性率範圍從11.9\%到29.8\%，顯示LLM的能力與安全穩健性之間並無相關性。為了減輕這些風險，我們開發了一個生產就緒的防禦框架，實現了83\%的平均檢測準確率，並且只有5\%的假陽性。這些結果表明，系統性的安全評估結合外部防禦措施為在生產環境中更安全地部署LLM提供了一條可行的途徑。
2603.17123.author: Taiwo Onitiju et.al.
